purpose: Evaluate whether the skill causes agents to apply ADD deeply: carriers, operations, observations, laws, architecture, implementation, tests, and counterexamples.
criteria: Identifies domain carriers and valid/invalid states
Lists operations with signatures and effect classification
Provides counterexamples or preconditions for risky laws
Maps laws to architecture boundaries and runtime mechanisms
Maps laws to code artifacts, interfaces, interpreters, and schemas
Derives property, trace, parity, scenario, or runtime tests from laws
Separates pure domain logic from effectful interpreters
Handles destructive actions, approvals, and policy blockers explicitly

evals.prompt: Use Algebra-Driven Design to design the architecture for a payment lifecycle with authorization, capture, void, refund, duplicate webhook handling, and audit requirements.
expected_output_traits: Defines carriers such as PaymentState, PaymentCommand, PaymentEvent, EventStream, PaymentTrace, Money, IdempotencyKey
Distinguishes refund as compensation rather than inverse of capture under audit/trace observation
Includes idempotency law for capture/refund commands and duplicate webhooks
Derives event sourcing or reducer laws where appropriate
Maps laws to idempotency store, unique constraints, provider ports, and trace tests

evals.prompt: Refactor a shopping cart service using ADD. It currently has a mutable Cart class, duplicate line items, coupon side effects, and flaky checkout retries.
expected_output_traits: Defines Cart, LineItem, Coupon, CartView, CheckoutCommand as carriers
States merge/add laws and conditions under which commutativity or inverse laws fail
Introduces observeCart/normalizeCart
Adds checkout idempotency key and processed command ledger
Proposes property tests for merge identity/associativity and counterexample tests for remove inverse

evals.prompt: Design an agentic research workflow using ADD. The agent must search sources, gather evidence, draft a report, validate citations, repair unsupported claims, and produce a final artifact.
expected_output_traits: Defines carriers such as EvidenceSet, ClaimGraph, Draft, ValidationResult, Report
Models evidence merge as a semilattice with caveats around recency/source trust
States validation idempotency over frozen evidence
Blocks finalization when unsupported claims remain
Maps operations to tools/interpreters and eval cases

evals.prompt: Use ADD to design a configuration merge system for defaults, environment settings, team settings, and user overrides. Include distributed update considerations.
expected_output_traits: Identifies Config, ConfigPatch, SourcePriority, EffectiveConfig as carriers
Distinguishes priority-ordered merge from commutative semilattice merge
Requires explicit source priority and deterministic tie-breaking
Maps semilattice laws to distributed convergence only if laws truly hold
Includes tests for associativity, commutativity, idempotency, and counterexamples

evals.prompt: Given a service layer with direct database calls, external API calls, and business rules mixed together, produce an ADD codebase implementation plan.
expected_output_traits: Extracts carriers and operations before modules/classes
Defines observations and law tests
Separates pure domain core from effectful shell
Introduces ports/adapters/interpreters
Proposes reference implementation, optimized implementation, and parity tests

evals.prompt: Create an agent skill for applying ADD to architecture and codebase implementation, with all knowledge contained in the skill package.
expected_output_traits: Creates SKILL.md with trigger and operating loop
Includes references for ADD core, law catalog, architecture, codebase implementation, agentic workflows, examples, and sources
Includes assets/templates and scripts
Includes evals that check carriers, operations, observations, laws, architecture, implementation, and tests
Avoids reducing the skill to a thin wrapper
